verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 encodes the type 1 IP3 receptor, a large homotetrameric calcium-release channel resident on the endoplasmic reticulum (and to a lesser extent the nuclear envelope). Its membrane topology places all functional domains—ligand-binding, regulatory, and channel-forming—on the cytoplasmic/ER-lumenal face, with no extracellular exposure at the plasma membrane. Checking each contextual bucket: (1) cell_state_induced — no evidence of stress/activation-induced PM surface display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — ER resident across all tissues, not a PM protein in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — not a lysosomal protein; (4) dual_localization — no documented stable PM pool detectable by surface biotinylation or flow on intact cells; (5) stable_surface_attachment — not secreted and no reported wash-resistant PM tethering. No therapeutic antibody/ADC/CAR-T program targets it at the cell surface. It is a canonical ER-resident intracellular channel; no isoform is membrane-anchored at the plasma membrane outer leaflet.
